# <C35> Wrapper Function

## Definition

<C35> is a wrapper function for <B35>. It calls <B35> internally and always returns the same value as <B35>, regardless of the input provided.

## Code Example

```python
def <C35>(x):
    return <B35>(x)

# Example usage with different inputs
result1 = <C35>(0)
result2 = <C35>(10)
result3 = <C35>(100)
```

## Key Insight

<C35> functions as an identity wrapper around <B35>. For any given input, calling <C35> produces exactly the same result as calling <B35> directly. This means <C35> adds no additional logic or transformation—it simply delegates to <B35> and passes through its return value unchanged.
